message:
feat(ft-xbnl0.2.3): cx-first send_text in launch_shell/agent_cx (tick 294)

Closes 4 maybe-cx seam holes in `restore_process.rs` cx-first methods
that were calling ambient `send_text` despite having `cx: &Cx` in scope:

**`launch_shell_cx` (2 sites):**

- L597: `self.wezterm.send_text(pane_id, &cd_cmd)` →
  `send_text_with_cx(cx, pane_id, &cd_cmd)`
- L604: `self.wezterm.send_text(pane_id, &exec_cmd)` →
  `send_text_with_cx(cx, pane_id, &exec_cmd)`

**`launch_agent_cx` (2 sites):**

- L649: `self.wezterm.send_text(pane_id, &cd_cmd)` →
  `send_text_with_cx(cx, pane_id, &cd_cmd)`
- L655: `self.wezterm.send_text(pane_id, &full_cmd)` →
  `send_text_with_cx(cx, pane_id, &full_cmd)`

These fns were cx-first at their signature (took `cx: &Cx`) and
already used `sleep_with_cx` internally, but the wezterm `send_text`
calls around those sleeps were ambient — the two `send_text` calls
bracketing each `sleep_with_cx` failed to propagate cx into the mux
RPC layer.

This means session restore's shell/agent launch commands now fully
thread cx through:
- send cd (cx-first mux send)
- sleep (cx-first)
- send exec / agent command (cx-first mux send)

A cx-cancel during session restore (e.g., during `ft restart`) now
cleanly aborts at any send boundary instead of blocking on the
pending mux RPC.

This pattern of "cx-first fn calling ambient inner dep" is the
maybe-cx seam-hole class — same as tick 281 (wait_for_with_cx poll
loops). Fixing them requires only renaming the call site since Cx is
already in scope.

Verification: `cargo check -p frankenterm --bin ft` compiles clean.

Cumulative ft-xbnl0.2.3 ticks this session: 108 (187-294).
frankenterm-core internal sites: 54 (prior 50 + restore_process 4).
main.rs cross-crate total: 192 sites (unchanged this tick).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
